options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Which condition(s) are required for victory. If score or both is chosen,
will turn on Scoresanity

options.name: scoresanity
options.description:
Earn checks for various score thresholds.

When using 'with_extra', one extra score check will be placed between checks
5-10, two extra score checks will be placed between checks 10-15, and three
extra checks will be placed between checks 15-20

options.name: scoresanity_difficulty
options.description:
How difficult the Scoresanity checks should be.

'Normal' is equivalent to a max score of 3000.

options.name: starting_max_fruit_size
options.display_name: Initial number of sizes of fruit
options.description: How many sizes of fruit to start with access to. Fewer starting fruit means fewer sphere 1 checks.

options.name: shuffle_fruit_order
options.description:
Shuffle the fruit order. If set to 'visual', the fruit will get bigger as
you progress. If 'on', the size of the fruit is also shuffled (this makes
the early game harder but may make the late game easier)

options.name: height_upgrade_count
options.description:
How many Progressive Field Height items to add into the pool. When set to 0,
disables the feature. When non-zero, the field starts at 50% of its normal
height and increases linearly to its normal height with each Progressive Field
Height

options.name: cooldown_upgrade_count
options.description:
How many Progressive Cooldown Reduction items to add into the pool. When set
to 0, disables the feature. When non-zero, you start with a cooldown between
each fruit drop, which reduces linearly to zero with each Progressive
Cooldown Reduction

options.name: starting_drop_cooldown
options.description:
How long your initial drop cooldown is, in seconds. Has no effect if
cooldown_upgrade_count is 0 (as that disables the feature entirely)

options.name: next_needs_unlock
options.description: Does the ability to see the next fruit need unlocking?

options.name: bonus_points_chance
options.description:
Chance, in percent, that any given filler item will be replaced with some
bonus points.

options.name: trap_chance
options.description: Chance, in percent, that any given filler item will be replaced with a trap.

options.name: insta_drop_trap_weight
options.description:
The relative weight that any given trap will be an Instant Drop Trap.

Note that this is relative to the weights of other traps; if you have
Instant Drop Trap set to 50, Shuffle Trap set to 25, and all other traps set
to 0, there will be a 2/3 chance of Instant Drop trap being selected when a
trap is generated

options.name: shuffle_trap_weight
options.description:
The relative weight that any given trap will be a Shuffle Trap.

Note that this is relative to the weights of other traps; if you have
Instant Drop Trap set to 50, Shuffle Trap set to 25, and all other traps set
to 0, there will be a 2/3 chance of Instant Drop trap being selected when a
trap is generated

options.name: impulse_trap_weight
options.description:
The relative weight that any given trap will be an Impulse Trap.

Note that this is relative to the weights of other traps; if you have
Instant Drop Trap set to 50, Shuffle Trap set to 25, and all other traps set
to 0, there will be a 2/3 chance of Instant Drop trap being selected when a
trap is generated

options.name: thanos_trap_weight
options.description:
The relative weight that any given trap will be a Thanos Trap.

Note that this is relative to the weights of other traps; if you have
Instant Drop Trap set to 50, Shuffle Trap set to 25, and all other traps set
to 0, there will be a 2/3 chance of Instant Drop trap being selected when a
trap is generated

options.name: secrecy_trap_weight
options.description:
The relative weight that any given trap will be a Secrecy Trap.

Note that this is relative to the weights of other traps; if you have
Instant Drop Trap set to 50, Shuffle Trap set to 25, and all other traps set
to 0, there will be a 2/3 chance of Instant Drop trap being selected when a
trap is generated

options.name: blind_trap_weight
options.description:
The relative weight that any given trap will be a Blindness Trap.

Note that this is relative to the weights of other traps; if you have
Instant Drop Trap set to 50, Shuffle Trap set to 25, and all other traps set
to 0, there will be a 2/3 chance of Instant Drop trap being selected when a
trap is generated

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
